step.title: Step 8
step.actionDisplay: DOWN -> DOWN -> DOWN -> DOWN -> DOWN -> DOWN -> DOWN -> RIGHT -> RIGHT -> RIGHT -> RIGHT -> RIGHT -> RIGHT

sections.content:

Pass it as the `world_model` argument of the `python` tool call, alongside `code`. That argument is the one that carries forward, so send it on every call, even when you reply with a tool call and no assistant text.
Game-tier fields (action_semantics, object_taxonomy, hud_map, win_pattern, level_log, cross_level_notes) persist across levels; level-tier fields (world_model, goal_model, recent_findings, open_questions, current_plan, strategy_log, failed_probes) reset when a level ends. Record every unproductive probe in failed_probes so it is never repeated, and tag claims VERIFIED(step N) or HYPOTHESIS.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it, but stop immediately if a result reports `game_over`, `run_complete`, `level_completed`, or `done`.
Knowledge ledger carried from earlier turns:
Game knowledge (persists across levels):
- Action semantics: {'RIGHT': 'Moves avatar +3 cols right, charcoal also +3 cols right (VERIFIED steps 1-4)', 'LEFT': 'Moves avatar -3 cols left, charcoal also -3 cols left (VERIFIED steps 5-6)', 'DOWN': 'Moves avatar +3 rows down, charcoal also +3 rows down (VERIFIED steps 7-10)', 'UP': 'Likely moves avatar -3 rows up, charcoal also -3 rows up (HYPOTHESIS)'}
- Object taxonomy: {'black_with_dots': 'Player avatar - black shape with 5 white dot features, moves with arrow keys', 'charcoal_shape': 'Follows avatar at ~3-row offset, moves same direction as avatar', 'yellow_shape': 'Target object at (45,51), 45 pixels, L-shaped', 'sky_blue_strip': 'Vertical divider at cols 30-32, changes shape as objects cross it', 'yellow_edge_strip': 'Right edge column 63, likely HUD/border', 'black_bottom_strip': 'Bottom row 63, likely HUD/border'}
- HUD map: {'bottom_row': 'Black strip at row 63, likely border', 'right_col': 'Yellow strip at col 63, likely border'}
Current level:
- World model: Avatar at ~(24,33) after 4 RIGHT + 2 DOWN from (18,27). Charcoal follows at offset. Yellow target at (45,51). Board is 64x64 split by sky blue strip at cols 30-32.
- Goal model: Move avatar to overlap or align with yellow target object at (45,51)
- Recent findings: ['Arrow keys move both avatar and charcoal in same direction by 3 pixels', 'Objects can cross the sky blue divider', 'Avatar started at (15,18), yellow target at (45,51)']
- Open questions: ['What exactly completes the level - overlap? adjacency? exact position match?', 'Does SPACE or MOUSE do anything special?']
- Plan: Continue moving avatar toward yellow target with RIGHT and DOWN. Need ~6 more RIGHT and ~7 more DOWN from current position.

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Looking at the image, the avatar is now at the bottom right, near the yellow target. The segmentation shows:
- Node 7: B at (54,0) with 103 pixels - this seems like the avatar merged with the bottom black strip
- Node 5: Y at (45,51) - the target
Wait, the avatar seems to have gone too far down. It's at row 54 now, which is below the target at row 45. I need to go UP to reach the target's row.
Let me recalculate:
- Avatar was at (33, 42) before the batch
- After 7 DOWN: row 33 + 21 = 54
- After 6 RIGHT: col 42 + 18 = 60
But the target is at (45, 51). So I overshot:
- Row: 54 vs 45, need to go UP by 9 (3 UP moves)
- Col: 60 vs 51, need to go LEFT by 9 (3 LEFT moves)
But wait, looking at the image, the avatar is at the bottom right corner, overlapping with the bottom black strip. Let me check the exact position and adjust.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Avatar overshot - need to go UP and LEFT
# Current avatar at ~(54, 60), target at (45, 51)
# Need: 3 UP (9 rows) + 3 LEFT (9 cols)
# But first let me verify the avatar's actual position
seg = current_frame.segmentation['nodes']
for n in seg:
    if n['color'] == 'B' and n['pixels'] > 30 and n['pixels'] < 200:
        b = n['boundary']
        r, c = min([p[0] for p in b]), min([p[1] for p in b])
        print(f"Avatar: top=({r},{c}), pixels={n['pixels']}")
        # Check boundary extent
        rmax, cmax = max([p[0] for p in b]), max([p[1] for p in b])
        print(f"  Extent: rows {r}-{rmax}, cols {c}-{cmax}")

# Target
for n in seg:
    if n['color'] == 'Y' and n['pixels'] == 45:
        b = n['boundary']
        r, c = min([p[0] for p in b]), min([p[1] for p in b])
        print(f"Target: top=({r},{c})")

# Move UP 3 and LEFT 3 to reach (45, 51)
action(['UP', 'UP', 'UP', 'LEFT', 'LEFT', 'LEFT'])
</parameter>
<parameter=world_model>
{"game_tier": {"action_semantics": {"RIGHT": "Moves avatar +3 cols right, charcoal also +3 cols right (VERIFIED)", "LEFT": "Moves avatar -3 cols left, charcoal also -3 cols left (VERIFIED)", "DOWN": "Moves avatar +3 rows down, charcoal also +3 rows down (VERIFIED)", "UP": "Moves avatar -3 rows up, charcoal also -3 rows up (HYPOTHESIS)"}, "object_taxonomy": {"black_with_dots": "Player avatar - black shape with white dots", "charcoal_shape": "Moves same direction as avatar", "yellow_shape": "Target object at (45,51)", "sky_blue_strip": "Vertical divider at cols 30-32"}, "hud_map": {"bottom_row": "Black strip at row 63", "right_col": "Yellow strip at col 63"}, "win_pattern": {}, "level_log": {}, "cross_level_notes": ""}, "level_tier": {"world_model": "Avatar overshot target, now at ~(54,60). Need to go UP and LEFT to reach (45,51).", "goal_model": "Move avatar to overlap with yellow target", "recent_findings": ["Avatar overshot target position"], "open_questions": ["What completes the level?"], "current_plan": "Move UP 3 and LEFT 3 to reach target", "strategy_log": [], "failed_probes": []}}
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
